mnemonics.title: Message Call Components
mnemonics.content: SENDER sends VALUE with PAYLOAD and GAS to RECIPIENT - SVPGR mnemonic: Sender Value Payload Gas Recipient

mnemonics.title: Call Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: CALL: Context Changes - DELEGATECALL: Storage Preserved - STATICCALL: No Changes Allowed. Remember: 'Static means stationary (no state change)'

mnemonics.title: Reentrancy Prevention
mnemonics.content: CEI - Checks-Effects-Interactions: Check conditions, Update Effects, then Interaction (external call). Prevents recursive attacks!

mnemonics.title: Fallback Order
mnemonics.content: When receiving Ether with data: 1) Matching function? Execute it. 2) No match? Fallback. When receiving Ether without data: 1) receive()? Execute it. 2) No receive()? Fallback (if payable).

acronyms.usage: Security pattern to prevent reentrancy: validate, update state, then call external contracts
User-controlled account with private key, can send message calls and create transactions
The payload sent in a message call: first 4 bytes = function selector, remaining = encoded arguments

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flow: Sender → [msg.sender=A, msg.value=10, calldata=func+args] → Recipient Contract → Execute → Return Data → Back to Sender. Show how msg values propagate inward but return data flows back.

Two boxes: Proxy Contract Storage (Slot 0: admin, Slot 1: data) | Implementation Code. When delegatecall runs, Implementation code writes to Proxy's storage slots - show arrows from Impl code to Proxy storage.
Stack diagram: Level 1 (external tx) → Level 2 (A calls B) → Level 3 (B calls C) → ... up to Level 1024. Show overflow error when exceeding 1024 levels.

analogies.analogy: Message Call is like sending a letter to an existing office (existing contract). Contract Creation is like building a NEW office building (new contract) - one-time setup.
Think of delegatecall like a power of attorney: the lawyer (delegatecall) uses YOUR resources (storage) to execute THEIR knowledge (code). The lawyer acts in your name with your assets.
Staticcall is like reading a book in a library - you can see and use the information but cannot annotate, dog-ear pages, or take the book (no state modifications allowed).

Imagine a vending machine that dispenses soda BEFORE checking if you paid enough. You could keep taking sodas until empty. The Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern is like the machine checking payment FIRST, then deducting from your account, THEN dispensing.
